metrics.narrative: Customer_Business_Problem__c and repeated call evidence name concrete functional pain (manual tender processes, no e-bidding in Ramco, slow sourcing execution) but no quantified business-impact figure (cycle time, cost, risk exposure) has been buyer-voiced. Business_Objectives__c itself notes 'discovery done partially in understanding their pain points.' The only numbers on record are Zycus's own commercial figures (proposal MYR 460k-980k/yr plus implementation), not a buyer ROI case.

champion.narrative: Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c = 'Siti'; SF role Decision Maker) was a strong, clearly pro-Zycus champion through late 2025 - 'strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti-Ramco' (10 Dec 2025 f2f) - and secured internal approval to move forward with Zycus modules (29 Jan 2026 Next Step). She has had no verified direct engagement since a brief email reply on 2026-03-30 ('Thanks for the proposal'), roughly 101 days ago, and does not appear by name in any Next Step update since the deal moved to the MMC group level (1 Jun-2 Jul 2026), raising real risk she has been bypassed as decision authority shifted upward.

competition.narrative: Ramco (the incumbent P2P system) is the dominant, well-evidenced threat: management flip-flopped between retaining it short-term (29 Jan 2026) and dropping it (9 Feb 2026) with no confirmed resolution since, while Ramco was independently reported as 'pushing them hard' and cancelling its own renewal meetings (Jan 2026 Next Step entries). SAP Ariba and iValua are logged in Competitors__c but no call or Next Step evidence corroborates active competition from either - likely names carried from an earlier general vendor scan (Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c notes 'Northport have evaluated vendors over the last 2-3 years').

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well evidenced and buyer-articulated: manual tendering with no e-bidding capability, Ramco P2P described by the buyer's own IT lead as urgent to move off ('it's quite urgent... we do things manually,' 25 Nov 2025 call), and repeated references to Ramco cancelling meetings and putting its own P2P renewal at risk. Customer_Business_Problem__c corroborates: manual processes, poor sourcing capability, RAMCO P2P legacy and unfriendly.

paper_process.narrative: Real paper has moved: Zycus's MSA was shared with Siti ahead of a 9 Mar 2026 workshop 'to save time if Northport decides to move forward' (25 Feb 2026 email), and a revised 2-phase commercial proposal (27 Mar 2026) tied discount eligibility to a 31 Mar 2026 LOI and MSA/Order Form execution by 15 Apr 2026. Both deadlines lapsed with only a brief, non-committal buyer reply ('Thanks for the proposal,' 30 Mar 2026). The paper process effectively reset when scope moved to the MMC group level in June.

economic_buyer.narrative: X10a_Sponsor__c names the role only ('Northport CFO'), never a person, and Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c is false. The Next Step trail names a real candidate EB, MMC group owner Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary, to whom a group-wide proposal was delivered on 2 Jul 2026 (~1 week ago) with a rep-reported 'positive response and level of interest' - but this is Zycus's own account, not a buyer quote from a call Zycus attended. Northport's 'new CEO' is also named (6 Apr 2026 Next Step) as needing to sign off on pricing comparisons, again with no direct Zycus engagement on record. No economic buyer at any level has appeared on an Avoma-captured call.

decision_process.narrative: The process has moved through at least two distinct gates: a 12 Mar 2026 internal Northport discussion 'with CFO/CIO/CPO', then a 6 Apr 2026 requirement that Agilaan Praemkumar present 2 of 3 vendor prices to a 'new CEO' for sign-off (a step Zycus was only told about after the fact). On 1 Jun 2026 the process was restructured entirely: the opportunity moved to a group-wide MMC entity, taking the proposition directly to the group owner rather than through Northport management alone - a major, still-unresolved shift in who actually decides.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria have been voiced across calls rather than documented in the BRD field (Business_Requirements__c is blank): end-to-end e-bidding from tender through award, Microsoft Teams-based intake, integration to Ramco via API for master data/PO/contract award, and iRisk scorecards preferred over 'Ramco's PO-level risk assessments' (30 Dec 2025 call). Budget constraints were explicit in scoping: iRisk and Anna (agentic AI) were held as optional add-ons in the 30 Dec 2025 planning call due to cost.

items.summary: Deal size nearly tripled, pointing to an expanded scope of the evaluation (e.g. more sites, modules, or users) rather than a like-for-like re-quote.

items.name: Forecast: Best Case → Pipeline
items.summary: Despite the stage and amount jump, forecast confidence was downgraded, indicating the bigger deal is seen as less certain to close — consistent with a reset evaluation rather than a straightforward upsell.

day_summary.overall: Big, contradictory day on paper: the deal advanced a stage to Formal Evaluation and the amount nearly tripled from 240K to 650K, but forecast was simultaneously downgraded from Best Case back to Pipeline — signalling this is a scope expansion still needing to be re-qualified, not a confirmed win. Combined with the prior slip from Vendor Selected back to Shortlisted and the close date pushed two months, the picture is a deal that got bigger but less certain, likely due to a re-scoped or re-opened evaluation on the buyer side. No inbound or outbound communication is recorded, so the driver behind these changes isn't documented — worth confirming what prompted the scope jump.

cro_panel.intro: A well-worked, deeply qualified regional deal (multiple demos, a technical workshop, phased commercial proposals, and MSA sharing) that has gone quiet at the buyer level for over three months just as the buying process was elevated to the ultimate MMC group owner.; The elevation could unlock a much larger, group-wide deal, but nothing has been confirmed and the original champion has not been heard from directly since a one-line email in March

The scope expanded from a Northport-only deal (~$240k) to a group-wide opportunity (~$150k/port x 7 = ~$1M), but the opp amount is still $650k and the budget is unconfirmed. The 30 Dec planning call noted budget constraints limited scope to core modules (iRisk and Anna held as optional). Without confirmed budget and EB sign-off, the deal cannot close.

The economic buyer is unmapped and unengaged. The Next Step names the MMC group owner and a Northport 'new CEO,' but neither is in contact roles or has appeared on calls. No CFO or finance executive is engaged on commercials, pricing, or ROI. The 12 Mar Next Step says 'internal discussion on next step (with CFO/CIO/CPO)' occurred, but it is unclear if this was a Zycus-attended meeting or internal buyer discussion, and no follow-up confirms EB engagement. Without EB sponsorship the deal cannot advance to the relevant stakeholder or close.

The buyer flip-flopped on the Ramco replacement decision: the 9 Feb Next Step states 'mgmt for Northport have decided to drop RAMCO now- flip flopped,' but the 29 Jan Next Step says 'mgmt is looking to retain RAMCO P2P, for short time while their contract expires.' The 20 Jan Next Step records 'Ramco pushing them hard (are aware to P2P side for them at risk).' The buyer's indecision and 3-month silence since 30 Mar suggest Ramco may have regained ground or internal alignment is unresolved.

The scope expanded from a Northport-only deal to a 7-port MMC group consolidation in June 2026 (Next Step 1 Jun: 'opportunity will move under a new entry for MMC- where we are trying to merge 7 ports and take a proposition directly to the owner'). This consolidation introduces multi-stakeholder approval complexity (budget tolerance checks across all 7 ports, group-level decision process) and decision delay. The opp amount ($650k) and stage (Formal Evaluation) do not reflect the expanded scope, and there is no evidence the consolidation has been formalized or the 7-port stakeholders aligned.

The buying process itself has been restructured mid-cycle, moving from a Northport management decision to a direct pitch to the ultimate group owner - a bigger prize, but a process reset with no fresh champion secured at that level yet.

The original LOI/MSA target (31 Mar/7 Apr 2026) lapsed roughly 13 weeks ago with no signature. flags: No quantified value case built — the buyer has articulated qualitative pain (manual tendering, no e-bidding) but no ROI, savings target, cycle-time reduction, or headcount impact has been captured. On a $650k-$1M deal (7-port consolidation), the EB will demand a business case to justify the budget. (Gap: quantified metrics and ROI; move: co-author a value case with Siti and finance, quantifying the cost of manual tendering and the savings/efficiency gain from Zycus S2P + AI, and use it to re-engage the EB.)

Buyer flip-flopped on Ramco replacement, signaling unresolved internal alignment — the 9 Feb Next Step states mgmt decided to drop Ramco, but the 29 Jan Next Step says mgmt is looking to retain Ramco P2P short-term, and the 20 Jan Next Step records 'Ramco pushing them hard.' The buyer's indecision and 3-month silence suggest Ramco may have regained ground or the decision is stalled. (Risk: Ramco retention; move: re-engage to confirm the Ramco replacement decision, validate the 7-port consolidation scope, and push the EB conversation to lock budget before Ramco's renewal cycle closes the window.)

Re-verify Ramco's current incumbent status before assuming displacement is locked in; the buyer flip-flopped on retaining vs. dropping Ramco as recently as Feb 2026 and Ramco was actively pushing its renewal.

Reconfirm Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus's role and access now that the decision has escalated above Northport to the MMC group level, since she has not been referenced in the deal record since it moved to the group entry.
Run a fresh Ramco competitive review before any group-wide commitment: Ramco actively pushed Northport in Jan 2026 and management flip-flopped on retaining it, with no resolution confirmed since.

Multi-thread named contacts at the other 6 MMC ports now, while the group-wide budget tolerance decision is still open, rather than relying solely on Northport-level relationships and partner-relayed updates.
Counter the logged 'high price tag' objection (Why_not_Zycus__c) with a group-wide TCO/ROI framing before the 31 Jul 2026 proposal-validity window lapses.
Convert the '1-2 weeks' verbal estimate on budget-tolerance confirmation into a specific, buyer-committed date.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal is technically well-qualified (deep discovery, a dedicated workshop, MSA shared, phased pricing) but has cooled at exactly the moment the buying process escalated to a much larger prize; the priority levers are re-anchoring the relationship at the new decision level and getting a hard date instead of a soft estimate.

customer_preference.evidence: The clearest buyer-voiced preference on record is from 10 Dec 2025 ('strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti-Ramco') - now over 200 days old and badly stale. The most recent signal (2 Jul 2026, MMC group owner 'positive response') is rep-reported, not a direct buyer quote from a call.

competitive_position.summary: Ramco, Northport's incumbent P2P/ERP system, is the only competitor with substantive, recent evidence behind it - and its status is genuinely unresolved after a management flip-flop in early 2026. SAP Ariba and iValua are logged in the CRM competitor field but have no corroborating call evidence; they read as a general market-scan artifact rather than an active threat.

The elevation could unlock a much larger, group-wide deal, but nothing has been confirmed and the original champion has not been heard from directly since a one-line email in March.

deal_risk.text: Group-wide budget tolerance across all 7 MMC ports remains unconfirmed; without it, the deal cannot close at the scale now being proposed.
Why_not_Zycus__c cites 'high price tag' and 'internal politics' as the buyer's stated objections - unaddressed since logged.
The original 17 Mar 2026 proposal states it is 'valid until 31 July 2026' - roughly three weeks from today - after which the commercial basis on the table lapses entirely.

win_position.text: Deep, multi-month qualification - discovery, a live demo, a dedicated 2-hour workshop, and a phased commercial proposal with MSA already shared - shows genuine buyer investment through Q1 2026 (Update Discussion 2026-02-11; Task 2026-02-25).

The MMC group owner, Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary, reportedly responded positively to the group-wide proposal on 2 Jul 2026, a potential access-to-power breakthrough if it converts to direct engagement.
No economic buyer at any level (Northport CFO, the 'new CEO', or the MMC group owner) has ever appeared on a Zycus-attended call; every EB signal is secondhand via the rep's Next Step notes.

The champion, Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus, has not been directly heard from since a one-line email on 30 Mar 2026, and is absent from every Next Step update since the deal moved to the group level in June.

deal_momentum.text: LastActivityDate is 2026-04-16 (84 days ago); the last verified two-way buyer touch was an incoming email on 2026-03-30 (101 days ago) - a rep follow-up on 16 Apr 2026 asking to schedule reference visits went unanswered.

Rep-reported forward motion exists via Next Step updates through 2 Jul 2026 (proposal delivered to the group owner, budget-tolerance confirmation now in motion), though none of this is yet corroborated by a logged call or email reply.
Both commercial forcing dates lapsed without buyer action: the 31 Mar 2026 LOI discount deadline and the 15 Apr 2026 MSA/Order Form execution date.
